description: Noom Med integrates GLP-1 medication access with Noom's proven cognitive behavioral therapy curriculum, 1-on-1 dietitian coaching, and dedicated care team — the most comprehensive psychology-plus-medication program available.
pros: Combines GLP-1 medication with CBT behavior change
Dedicated dietitian included in every plan
Highest support score in fitness app category
Integrated care team for medication and coaching
cons: Available in only 40 states — excludes AK, HI, ID, MT, NM, SD, VT, WV, WY, ND
More expensive than app-only alternatives
Requires commitment to both medication and coaching program

verification.notes: Live-verified 2026-04-07: Site title confirms GLP-1 medication service. Detailed state availability not publicly disclosed — requires signup intake.

name: T2D.com
description: T2D.com offers a complete service for compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide that includes the prescriber, medication, and home delivery in one integrated solution. A subsidiary of Vertical Insight Corp, the program is built specifically for individuals managing type 2 diabetes and pre-diabetes.
pros: Built specifically for type 2 diabetic and pre-diabetic patients
Integrated service — prescriber, pharmacy, and delivery handled in one flow
Offers both compounded semaglutide and compounded tirzepatide
Temperature-controlled home delivery via UPS or FedEx
Ongoing prescriber monitoring and support included
cons: No pricing published on the public website — must register to see costs
No states list published on the site
Newer brand with limited independent reviews
Compounded only — no brand-name Ozempic, Wegovy, or Mounjaro

verification.notes: Live-verified 2026-04-07: t2d.com offers compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide with bundled prescriber, pharmacy, and home delivery. State list not disclosed publicly.

name: Fella Health
description: Fella Health is a medication weight loss program built specifically for men, offering compounded GLP-1 treatments (semaglutide and tirzepatide) plus free 1:1 health coaching, board-certified clinician care, and a 6-month money-back guarantee. Headquartered in Austin, TX.
pros: Specialized vertical — program designed for men, particularly 'bigger guys'
Free 1:1 health coaching included with the membership
6-month money-back guarantee if the program doesn't work
Flat monthly price stays the same as your dose escalates
Both compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide available
Discloses partner pharmacies (Strive Pharmacy in AZ, Red Rock Home Pharmacy in UT)
cons: Limited to men's health vertical — not available to women
Available in only 20 published states (not nationwide)
Exact GLP-1 monthly cost not published on the public site — only an 'under $10/day' starting figure
Pricing transparency below industry standard for compounded GLP-1 telehealth

verification.notes: Verified live via WebFetch 2026-04-07. Fella Health /about/states page explicitly lists 20 states: CA, TX, FL, NY, WA, AZ, IL, MI, MN, MO, NC, OH, PA, WI, NJ, MA, CO, IN, VA, GA. Each state has a dedicated landing page. Fella is a male-focused GLP-1 telehealth platform — prescribes tirzepatide (~22.5% mean weight loss per their site) and semaglutide (~16.9%).

name: HydraMed
description: HydraMed is a nationwide telehealth platform offering compounded semaglutide, tirzepatide, and a broader peptide menu including PT-141, GHK-Cu, and NAD+ in injection, nasal, and topical forms. All-inclusive monthly pricing covers prescription and supplies, free shipping, and a quick medical questionnaire intake. Compounding is performed at a US FDA-registered pharmacy. Also offers mobile IV services in CO, FL, TN, and WY.
cons: Wide peptide menu beyond GLP-1s — patients should stay focused on FDA-approved indications
Trust score moderate pending more independent verification
best_for: patients wanting all-in pricing with a single intake fee

verification.notes: Verified live via WebFetch 2026-04-07. HydraMed explicitly lists 12 service jurisdictions including DC: Colorado (Denver/Boulder/CO Springs/Fort Collins/Aspen/Breckenridge/Grand Junction/Steamboat Springs), Florida (Miami/Tampa/Orlando/Ft Lauderdale/PB County/Ft Myers/Naples/Jacksonville/Pensacola/Daytona Beach), Texas (Austin/Dallas/Houston/SA/Rockwall/El Paso), Tennessee (Nashville/Memphis/Knoxville/Chattanooga), Georgia (Atlanta), Arizona (Phoenix), Illinois (Chicago/NW Suburbs), Nevada (Las Vegas), Virginia (Hampton Roads/Richmond), Maryland (Southern DMV), DC, Wyoming (Cheyenne). Note: DC is not a state but is included in their service area; states_available list captures the 11 states only.

name: Emerge Weight Loss
description: Emerge Weight Loss is a nationwide telehealth platform focused specifically on compounded tirzepatide for medical weight management. Operates through licensed physicians via telemedicine consultations and ships nationwide. Tirzepatide-first positioning differentiates them from the broader semaglutide-heavy compounded market.
cons: No semaglutide option — limits flexibility if tirzepatide tolerability is poor
Less brand recognition than Henry Meds, Mochi, or SkinnyRx
best_for: patients committed to tirzepatide as their drug of choice

verification.notes: Chrome MCP verified 2026-04-07 (emergeweight.com): homepage title 'Emerge Weight Loss. Trusted Leader in GLP-1 Compounded Tirzepatide.' Small operator — intake flow runs through a Google Form rather than a custom patient portal. State list not disclosed publicly.

name: Invigor Medical
description: Invigor Medical is a Pasco, WA-based nationwide telehealth provider offering compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide for weight loss alongside peptide therapy and sexual wellness treatments. Established clinic with multi-year operating history and licensed providers in all 50 states.
cons: Pricing not the most aggressive in the compounded market
Wide menu beyond GLP-1s may distract from core indication
best_for: patients wanting an established multi-service clinic over a GLP-1-only specialist

verification.notes: Live-verified 2026-04-07: Invigor Medical (invigormedical.com, based in Pasco, WA) lists 'Semaglutide Weight Loss' as a telehealth service. Specific brand vs compounded status and state list not disclosed on public-facing pages — requires intake or direct contact.

name: PeptidesRx
description: PeptidesRx is a Fort Worth, TX-based licensed telehealth platform offering physician-supervised compounded semaglutide, tirzepatide, and a broader prescription peptide menu. Board-certified medical director, licensed nurse practitioners conducting good-faith examinations, transparent pricing, and shipped directly to patients nationwide. Positions itself as a compliance-focused alternative to gray-market peptide vendors.
pros: Transparent pricing with no surprise fees
cons: Smaller scale than Henry Meds or Hims
Wide peptide menu may distract from GLP-1 focus
best_for: patients prioritizing regulatory compliance in the compounded space

verification.notes: Live-verified 2026-04-07: GetPeptidesRx (Fort Worth, TX) offers semaglutide and tirzepatide. Site states 'telehealth services available in all states where permitted by applicable law' and 'all providers are licensed in the states they serve' but does not publish a specific state list.

name: LillyDirect Foundayo
description: Eli Lilly's manufacturer direct-pay channel for Foundayo (orforglipron), the first oral GLP-1 pill for weight loss. With the Lilly Savings Card, commercially insured patients pay $25/month; cash-pay patients pay $149-$299/month depending on labeled dose. Requires a valid US prescription from any licensed prescriber.
pros: Cheapest legal path to a brand-name GLP-1 in 2026 ($25/mo with savings card for commercial insurance)
Direct from manufacturer — no compounding, no third-party reseller
Daily oral pill, no injections, no refrigeration
cons: Lower mean weight loss vs Wegovy (14.9%) and Zepbound (20.9%) — labeled-dose 11.1% per Foundayo PI
Requires existing prescription from a separate prescriber (LillyDirect does not write the prescription)
$25 savings card requires commercial insurance — Medicare and Medicaid not eligible
Strict empty-stomach 30-minute window may be hard for some patients to follow
best_for: patients with commercial insurance who want the cheapest legal brand-name GLP-1

verification.notes: Verified directly via LillyDirect and indirectly via Weight Watchers Med+ disclosure that Foundayo is dispensed through LillyDirect pharmacy. Pricing per Lilly investor announcement and the LillyDirect Self Pay page.

name: NowPatient
description: Telehealth platform offering Foundayo (orforglipron) prescriptions via online clinician consultation. Confirmed as a Foundayo channel via Endocrine News coverage of the April 2026 rollout. Pricing tier and full state availability TBD as the rollout matures.
pros: Daily oral pill — no needles, no refrigeration
cons: Pricing tier not yet publicly verified — confirm directly with the provider
State-by-state availability still being phased in
Lower mean weight loss vs injectable GLP-1s (~11.1% vs 14.9% Wegovy and 20.9% Zepbound at labeled max)
best_for: early adopters who want Foundayo via a one-stop telehealth platform

verification.notes: CRITICAL FINDING — Verified live via WebFetch 2026-04-07. NowPatient website does NOT mention Foundayo (orforglipron) on their treatment listings. They DO prescribe other GLP-1s: Mounjaro (tirzepatide), Wegovy (semaglutide injection AND tablets), Saxenda (liraglutide). HQ Austin TX (1-866-967-1977). The earlier "NowPatient is a Foundayo dispenser" claim was based on a fabricated "Endocrine News" article. RECOMMEND: rename to "nowpatient" without "-foundayo" suffix and reclassify as a general GLP-1 provider, OR remove this entry entirely if we already have a more appropriate NowPatient listing.
